jokes.text: Do you like dane cook? / yeah, he's awesome. / so far so good.
jokes.setup: Jeff asks about Dane Cook to gauge the student's taste level
jokes.punchline: The positive response confirms Jeff found the right easy class with low standards
jokes.explanation: Efficient character establishment - Jeff's calculating nature and the absurd academic standards at Greendale. Dane Cook as comedy litmus test is clever.

jokes.text: Now...close them and throw them away. Throw it away! [laughs] Throw it away!
jokes.setup: Opens textbooks like a normal class
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts by demanding students throw books away
jokes.explanation: Classic Dead Poets Society parody setup. Whitman's manic energy sells the absurdity of the anti-academic stance.

jokes.text: You, that mole is raised and dark. It's not a good combo.
jokes.setup: Inspirational carpe diem speech about mortality
jokes.punchline: Casually suggests student might have skin cancer
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - takes 'seize the day because you might die' to uncomfortable specificity. Great tonal whiplash.

jokes.text: Voice command! [beep] / would you please use the buttons? / okay, grandpa.
jokes.setup: Pierce struggling with voice commands repeatedly
jokes.punchline: Annie's exasperation leads to Pierce's dismissive ageism comeback
jokes.explanation: Solid character beat establishing Pierce's tech incompetence and casual insensitivity. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: [high-pitched sneeze] / oh, god bless you, dear. [laughter] / what's so funny?
jokes.setup: Troy sneezes in a high-pitched, feminine way
jokes.punchline: Group's laughter at the unexpected sound, Troy's defensive confusion
jokes.explanation: Sets up Troy's masculinity insecurities. Simple physical comedy that works through surprise and character.

jokes.text: Troy's high-pitched sneeze followed by laughter
jokes.setup: Troy sneezes
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly high-pitched, feminine sneeze from the former jock
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy that contrasts with Troy's athletic image - sets up his insecurity

jokes.text: 9/11 was pretty much the 9/11 of the falafel business.
jokes.setup: Explaining his family restaurant's struggles since 2001
jokes.punchline: Compares 9/11's impact on Middle Eastern businesses to 9/11 itself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy structure using comparison format. Abed's deadpan delivery of uncomfortable truth about post-9/11 prejudice. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: then he should see aladdin. jafar was a bad-ass. / yes, he was.
jokes.setup: Discussion about negative Arab stereotypes in media
jokes.punchline: Troy suggests Aladdin as counterpoint, citing the villain as positive representation
jokes.explanation: Troy's well-meaning but clueless attempt at cultural sensitivity. Abed's deadpan agreement adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: They're not as understanding as christians. You can get your head cut off with a salami sword.
jokes.setup: Warning about hardcore Muslim fathers
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific and nonsensical 'salami sword' threat
jokes.explanation: Shirley's casual racism delivered through absurd imagery. 'Salami sword' is memorably ridiculous while highlighting her prejudices.

jokes.text: That's the most racist thing i've ever heard. / pierce will beat that in one minute. [watch beeps]
jokes.setup: Britta calls out Shirley's racist comment
jokes.punchline: Jeff predicts Pierce will say something worse, watch times it perfectly
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy establishing Pierce's reputation for offensive comments. The watch timing adds perfect comedic precision.

jokes.text: hey, troy sneezes like a girl. / and how about i pound you like a boy? That didn't come out right.
jokes.setup: Pierce mocks Troy's feminine sneeze
jokes.punchline: Troy's aggressive comeback has unintended sexual implications
jokes.explanation: Troy's masculinity insecurity creates accidental innuendo. Relies somewhat on cheap sexual humor but fits character.

jokes.text: you're not my mother. / she's not? / 29 seconds.
jokes.setup: Troy rejects Shirley's mothering, Pierce asks if she's actually his mother
jokes.punchline: Jeff counts down to Pierce's predicted racist comment
jokes.explanation: Pierce's oblivious question and Jeff's countdown maintain comedic tension. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: because i wasted 15 years of my life on a man Who left me with nothing but stretch marks And a foggy memory of two bland orgasms.
jokes.setup: Professor asks why she's at Greendale, expecting simple answer
jokes.punchline: Shirley overshares intimate details about her failed marriage and sex life
jokes.explanation: Shirley's unexpected vulgarity creates great character moment. Cringe comedy works through contrast with her usual sweet demeanor.

jokes.text: Why did the pretty young girl die alone surrounded by sweater-wearing cats Who are trained to use human toilets?
jokes.setup: Giving Annie a 'quiz' about her life choices
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific vision of her future as crazy cat lady with toilet-trained cats
jokes.explanation: Whitman's poetic absurdity at its peak. The toilet-trained cats detail elevates a cliche 'dying alone' prediction.

jokes.text: [desk collapses] - ooh! ooh! oh, heaven. My.
jokes.setup: Standing on desks to 'rise above programming'
jokes.punchline: Annie's desk immediately collapses, undermining the inspirational moment
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy undercutting pretentious moment. Whitman's poetic reaction to the injury adds character humor.

jokes.text: oh, yeah. his name's pierce. I-i can talk to him for you.
jokes.setup: Professor identifies one student who's just coasting for easy grade
jokes.punchline: Jeff immediately deflects to Pierce instead of admitting it's him
jokes.explanation: Jeff's quick thinking and deflection perfectly demonstrates his manipulative nature. Clean misdirection joke.

jokes.text: I-i love you. Oh, there's one down. nine to go.
jokes.setup: Jeff tries to convince professor he's changed by saying he loves him
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals it's just homework assignment calculation
jokes.explanation: Jeff's cynical approach to emotional assignment creates perfect character comedy. Structure of fake emotion followed by calculation is clean.

jokes.text: This is no way to teach accounting!
jokes.setup: Jeff frustrated with unorthodox teaching methods
jokes.punchline: Calls it accounting instead of film class, revealing his complete disengagement
jokes.explanation: Jeff's complete disconnection from what class he's even in. Simple but effective character beat.

jokes.text: it is not fair how hard it is for you to cheat here.
jokes.setup: Jeff complaining about his academic situation
jokes.punchline: Britta sarcastically points out the absurdity of complaining about cheating difficulty
jokes.explanation: Britta's dry observation about Jeff's moral failings. Good character voice but not exceptional.

jokes.text: They're like real movies, but with ugly people.
jokes.setup: Explaining what documentaries are
jokes.punchline: Reduces the art form to 'real movies with ugly people'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed line - clinical film analysis mixed with casual brutality. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: holy...war.
jokes.setup: Sees Abed's father approaching angrily
jokes.punchline: Stops mid-'holy shit' to make Arabic/religious conflict pun
jokes.explanation: Jeff's quick wordplay shows his wit under pressure. The religious/cultural reference adds layers but risks insensitivity.

jokes.text: where do i find mr. britta? / i'm mr. britta. That's right. i'm a woman with rights. And you can see my whole face.
jokes.setup: Dad asks for Mr. Britta assuming masculine name
jokes.punchline: Britta aggressively asserts her gender and Western freedoms
jokes.explanation: Britta's confrontational feminism and cultural assumptions create good character clash. The face comment escalates unnecessarily.

jokes.text: Because i'm arab, i must hate women. Let me tell you something, i love women. But i'm getting a major b-word vibe from you.
jokes.setup: Dad calls out Britta's prejudiced assumptions
jokes.punchline: Defends his respect for women then immediately calls her a bitch
jokes.explanation: Good irony as Dad contradicts his defense of women. The 'b-word' softening adds to the contradiction.

jokes.text: dreams are for sleeping. / you don't know that. / it's clinically proven. / so's polio.
jokes.setup: Dad dismisses dreams, Britta challenges the claim
jokes.punchline: Britta's nonsensical polio comparison as counterargument
jokes.explanation: Britta's argument makes no logical sense but has comedic rhythm. The polio comparison is memorably absurd.

jokes.text: you lost me!
jokes.setup: Britta's incomprehensible polio argument
jokes.punchline: Dad's honest confusion at her logic
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to Britta's nonsensical argument. Simple but effective comedic beat.

jokes.text: i don't think i'm really in the scene.
jokes.setup: Family argument happening around him
jokes.punchline: Abed treats real life like a film production he's observing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed meta-commentary. His detachment from emotional family drama through film lens is brilliant character work.

jokes.text: Why are you dressed like an '80s rapist?
jokes.setup: Seeing Jeff in unusual outfit
jokes.punchline: Specific, disturbing pop culture reference for Jeff's appearance
jokes.explanation: Abed's brutal honesty with oddly specific cultural reference. Dark but memorable and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: um, all money is spending money.
jokes.setup: Britta mentions Abed needs spending money
jokes.punchline: Annie's logical observation that all money is for spending
jokes.explanation: Annie's practical, logical mind deflating Britta's dramatic gesture. Simple but effective character moment.

jokes.text: Bet that doesn't happen to you a lot. / happened yesterday.
jokes.setup: Jeff assumes Shirley rarely gets thanked
jokes.punchline: Shirley matter-of-factly contradicts his assumption
jokes.explanation: Shirley's dry response deflates Jeff's condescending assumption. Good character beat but not exceptional.

jokes.text: hillary, thank you for getting people coffee. I love you.
jokes.setup: Jeff continuing his homework to tell 10 people he loves them
jokes.punchline: Generic love declaration to random coffee server he calls wrong name
jokes.explanation: Continuation of Jeff's cynical approach to the assignment. Functional running gag but predictable.

jokes.text: boo. an ordinary coffee for an ordinary life.
jokes.setup: Jeff orders plain black coffee
jokes.punchline: Abed connects boring drink choice to boring existence
jokes.explanation: Abed's artistic pretension and judgment of Jeff's choices. Good character voice but not exceptional.

jokes.text: shazbot!
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to appear spontaneous for Professor Whitman
jokes.punchline: Uses dated Mork & Mindy catchphrase as fake spontaneity
jokes.explanation: Jeff's calculated 'spontaneity' using outdated reference. Shows his disconnect from genuine emotion but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: You look like a high school bitch.
jokes.setup: Pierce offering to help Troy with his image
jokes.punchline: Brutal assessment of Troy's letterman jacket
jokes.explanation: Pierce's blunt cruelty disguised as help. Character-appropriate but relies on mean humor.

jokes.text: i've seen men in your situation, troy. No, those are both for me.
jokes.setup: Pierce giving Troy advice, seems to be setting up profound wisdom
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he's talking about ordering two pieces of cake
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up serious mentor moment then reveals Pierce's gluttony instead

jokes.text: [deafening sneeze] [shouts incoherently]
jokes.setup: Pierce demonstrating 'masculine' sneezes
jokes.punchline: Absurdly loud, aggressive sneeze demonstration
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of Pierce's ridiculous sneeze coaching. Builds on established running gag but not sophisticated.

jokes.text: And maybe i wouldn't sneeze because i'm in control.
jokes.setup: Pierce explaining different sneeze techniques
jokes.punchline: Claims controlling sneezes shows dominance
jokes.explanation: Pierce's bizarre masculine philosophy about bodily functions. Good character work showing his odd worldview.

jokes.text: Watch the kite. uh-oh. Mind if i get a chance? oh! Look at this. one foot. [laughing]
jokes.setup: Jeff's elaborate performance of spontaneous joy for professor
jokes.punchline: Painfully obvious staging of 'candid' life-seizing moments
jokes.explanation: Jeff's calculated spontaneity creates effective cringe comedy. The artificiality is painful to watch.

jokes.text: sloppy. And considering the age of these girls, Unwittingly creepy.
jokes.setup: Jeff's performance with young students
jokes.punchline: Professor notes the inappropriate optics of Jeff's behavior
jokes.explanation: Whitman's observation about Jeff's failed performance adds uncomfortable truth. Good character insight.

jokes.text: How long does it take you in the morning To--to make it look like you have bed head?
jokes.setup: Professor calling out Jeff's artificiality
jokes.punchline: Specific observation about Jeff's carefully crafted 'casual' appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect read of Jeff's character - exposes his vanity masquerading as effortlessness

jokes.text: How many sweat pants, sport jacket combos did you try Before you found the one that said 'i don't care'?
jokes.setup: Professor analyzing Jeff's calculated appearance
jokes.punchline: Calls out the effort Jeff puts into looking effortless
jokes.explanation: Brilliant insight into Jeff's vanity and calculated image. Whitman sees through Jeff's facade perfectly.

jokes.text: every time i try to ask him about it, He just keeps filming me and telling me That i'm playing the role of his mother.
jokes.setup: Britta frustrated with Abed's behavior
jokes.punchline: Abed treats real conversations as film scenes with assigned roles
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed characterization - his inability to separate film from reality creates unique comedy and character depth.

jokes.text: abed is not normal. That's what you bought When you intentionally tangled yourself in his life.
jokes.setup: Britta complaining about Abed's strange behavior
jokes.punchline: Jeff points out she chose to get involved with someone clearly not normal
jokes.explanation: Jeff's logical but harsh assessment of Britta's situation. Good character insight about consequences.

jokes.text: What in the hell? Is he gonna kill us?
jokes.setup: Seeing Pierce's aggressive sneeze demonstration
jokes.punchline: Jeff's fear that Pierce's sneeze technique might be dangerous
jokes.explanation: Jeff's reasonable fear of Pierce's extreme masculinity lessons. Functional reaction comedy.

jokes.text: All i want to do is take care of you. Why won't you answer me? / 'cause this is the scene where you leave.
jokes.setup: Britta's emotional plea for communication
jokes.punchline: Abed reduces it to predictable film scene structure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy as Abed treats real emotion as film cliche. His detachment creates both humor and pathos.

jokes.text: someone order seven lattes? / i got that. / no, you don't got it. I got it, abed. i got everything.
jokes.setup: Simple coffee order interaction
jokes.punchline: Britta's aggressive takeover reveals her controlling nature
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Britta's need to control Abed extending to everything - character consistency

jokes.text: i think you are really weird, abed. And i think the wrong person just left.
jokes.setup: Abed asks for Jeff's opinion after Britta leaves
jokes.punchline: Jeff honestly calls Abed weird and suggests Britta wasn't the problem
jokes.explanation: Jeff's blunt honesty provides necessary reality check. Good character moment showing Jeff's directness.

jokes.text: perfect. that's a wrap.
jokes.setup: Emotional confrontation concludes
jokes.punchline: Abed treats it as successful film completion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - his satisfaction with capturing authentic emotion for his film. Meta-commentary on documentary ethics.

jokes.text: everyone should always do whatever they want and leave each other out of it.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to mediate between Abed's father and Britta
jokes.punchline: Proposes completely antisocial solution as universal wisdom
jokes.explanation: Jeff's selfish worldview presented as helpful advice. Good character consistency with his avoidance issues.

jokes.text: You and your pillow-lipped girlfriend Got all up in my stuff because you wanted to be cowboys
jokes.setup: Dad's angry response to Jeff's mediation
jokes.punchline: Bizarre 'pillow-lipped' insult and cowboy metaphor
jokes.explanation: Dad's creative insult and metaphor show his personality. 'Pillow-lipped' is memorably weird.

jokes.text: hadn't seen the iraq metaphor, but-- / what iraq metaphor? I am talking about your speeches And her guided--
jokes.setup: Jeff thinks dad is making Iraq War analogy
jokes.punchline: Dad genuinely meant literal guided missiles, not metaphor
jokes.explanation: Jeff's over-analysis meets Dad's literal thinking. Good cultural miscommunication comedy.

jokes.text: i don't want to be your father. / i think you are weird, abed.
jokes.setup: Abed's documentary using real audio from earlier
jokes.punchline: Jeff's actual rejection becomes his character's dialogue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant documentary structure using real dialogue as scripted performance. Meta-commentary on truth in film.

jokes.text: [crying]
jokes.setup: Watching Abed's documentary about their relationship
jokes.punchline: Tough father reduced to tears by son's film
jokes.explanation: Emotional payoff of father seeing himself through son's eyes. More touching than funny but effective.

jokes.text: [whispering] i made this all happen. / with falafel as a fallback.
jokes.setup: Britta taking credit for emotional reconciliation
jokes.punchline: Abed notes the practical compromise undermines her idealism
jokes.explanation: Abed deflating Britta's self-congratulation with practical reality. Good character dynamic.

jokes.text: well, britta, it isn't called friend business, It's called show business.
jokes.setup: Britta criticizing Abed's manipulation of friends
jokes.punchline: Abed's cold justification using show business cliche
jokes.explanation: Abed's defense of using friends for art through clever wordplay. Shows his priorities and detachment.

jokes.text: [clamorous sneeze] / god--god bless you. god bless you.
jokes.setup: Troy's new masculine sneeze technique
jokes.punchline: Absurdly loud sneeze gets repeated blessing
jokes.explanation: Payoff to Pierce's sneeze training. Physical comedy but not particularly clever or surprising.

jokes.text: hey, um, you should kiss me right now.
jokes.setup: Jeff needs to seize the day for his assignment
jokes.punchline: Directly propositions random woman for homework
jokes.explanation: Jeff's calculated romantic gesture for academic credit. Character-consistent manipulation but predictable.

jokes.text: day seized! / we're even. / [laughs] 'a' plus, winger.
jokes.setup: Jeff successfully kisses stranger
jokes.punchline: Professor witnesses and grades the calculated spontaneity positively
jokes.explanation: Ironic success of Jeff's fake spontaneity. Professor's enthusiasm for calculated behavior is amusing.

jokes.text: yeah. fooled you.
jokes.setup: Jeff appears to have genuinely seized the day
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was all calculated manipulation
jokes.explanation: Jeff's final admission that none of his growth was real. Perfect character consistency but somewhat predictable.
